**The Moon Keeper's Lantern**

Long ago, when the world was newly formed, the Sun blazed constantly in the sky, never resting. The creatures below grew exhausted, for there was no night, no time to sleep or dream.

A young girl named Sera climbed the highest mountain, carrying only a simple lantern. She called out to the Sun, "Great light, you work too hard. Please, rest sometimes."

The Sun replied, "If I rest, the world will fall into eternal darkness. Who will guide the lost?"

Sera lifted her lantern. "I will keep watch while you sleep."

Moved by her courage, the Sun agreed to set each evening. But Sera's small lantern could only hold so much oil. She filled it completely each month, creating the full moon's brilliant glow. As the oil burned through the nights, her light gradually dimmed, waning to a thin crescent.

When the lantern emptied, Sera would descend the mountain in darkness to refill it, creating the new moon. Then she'd climb again, and the cycle would begin anew.

To this day, Sera keeps her ancient promise, her faithful lantern waxing and waning across the night sky, guiding all who wander in darkness.

Long ago, when the sky was an empty black canvas, there lived a celestial weaver named Astria. She spent her days creating magnificent tapestries from threads of pure light, each one more beautiful than the last. But no matter how stunning her creations, they would fade and disappear into nothingness, for there was nowhere to display them in the void above.

One day, heartbroken by the impermanence of her art, Astria wept. Her tears floated upward, and where each teardrop touched the darkness, it transformed into a brilliant point of light that remained fixed in place. Amazed, she realized her sorrow had created something eternal.

Inspired, Astria began to work differently. She abandoned her loom and instead used her needle to pierce the veil of night. Through each tiny hole, she threaded drops of her luminous tears, creating patterns across the heavens. She formed great beasts, heroes, and symbols—a celestial gallery for all to see.

When her work was complete, Astria smiled and faded away, becoming one with her creation. Now, every night, we see her masterpiece: the stars, each one a permanent reminder that even from sorrow, beauty can emerge and shine forever.

In despair, she looked at the stars she'd created—thousands of frozen songs still shimmering. An idea formed. She stopped trying to sing and instead began to listen. From the heavens came a gentle humming: her old songs, still vibrating through space, echoing back to her.

The greatest acts of creation, Sera had taught him, sometimes require only careful attention and the courage to let others shine.
